name: django-admin
description: Utility script for the Django Web framework

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Usage and help information for django-admin

suggestions.name: changepassword
suggestions.description: Change a user's password for django.contrib.auth

suggestions.name: createsuperuser
suggestions.description: Used to create a superuser

suggestions.name: check
suggestions.description: Checks the entire Django project for potential problems

suggestions.name: compilemessages
suggestions.description: Compiles .po files to .mo files for use with builtin gettext support

suggestions.name: createcachetable
suggestions.description: Creates the tables needed to use the SQL cache backend

suggestions.name: dbshell
suggestions.description: Runs the command-line client for specified database, or the default database

suggestions.name: diffsettings
suggestions.description: Displays differences between the current settings.py and Django's default settings

suggestions.name: dumpdata
suggestions.description: Output the contents of the database as a fixture of the given format (using each model's default manager unless --all is specified)

suggestions.name: flush
suggestions.description: Removes ALL DATA from the database, including data added during migrations

suggestions.name: inspectdb
suggestions.description: Introspects the database tables in the given database and outputs a Django

suggestions.name: loaddata
suggestions.description: Installs the named fixture(s) in the database

suggestions.name: makemessages
suggestions.description: Runs over the entire source tree of the current directory and pulls out all strings marked for translation. It creates (or updates) a message file in the conf/locale (in the django tree) or locale (for projects and applications) directory. You must run this command with one of either the --locale, --exclude, or --all options

suggestions.name: makemigrations
suggestions.description: Creates new migration(s) for apps

suggestions.name: migrate
suggestions.description: Updates database schema. Manages both apps with migrations and those without

suggestions.name: sendtestemail
suggestions.description: Sends a test email to the email addresses specified as arguments

suggestions.name: shell
suggestions.description: Runs a Python interactive interpreter. Tries to use IPython or bpython, if one of them is available. Any standard input is executed as code

suggestions.name: showmigrations
suggestions.description: Shows all available migrations for the current project

suggestions.name: sqlflush
suggestions.description: Returns a list of the SQL statements required to return all tables in the database to the state they were in just after they were installed

suggestions.name: sqlmigrate
suggestions.description: Prints the SQL statements for the named migration

suggestions.name: sqlsequencereset
suggestions.description: Prints the SQL statements for resetting sequences for the given app name(s)

suggestions.name: squashmigrations
suggestions.description: Squashes an existing set of migrations (from first until specified) into a single new one

suggestions.name: startapp
suggestions.description: Creates a Django app directory structure for the given app name in the current directory or optionally in the given directory

suggestions.name: startproject
suggestions.description: Creates a Django project directory structure for the given project name in the current directory or optionally in the given directory

suggestions.name: test
suggestions.description: Discover and run tests in the specified modules or the current directory

suggestions.name: testserver
suggestions.description: Runs a development server with data from the given fixture(s)

suggestions.name: clearsessions
suggestions.description: Can be run as a cronjob or directly to clean out expired sessions (only with the database backend at the moment)

suggestions.name: collectstatic
suggestions.description: Collect static files in a single location

suggestions.name: findstatic
suggestions.description: Finds the absolute paths for the given static file(s)

suggestions.name: runserver
suggestions.description: Starts a lightweight Web server for development and also serves static files

options.name: --all
options.description: Display all settings, regardless of their value. In "hash" mode, default values are prefixed by "###"

options.name: --default
options.description: The settings module to compare the current settings against. Leave empty to compare against Django's default settings

options.name: --output
options.description: Selects the output format. 'hash' mode displays each changed setting, with the settings that don't appear in the defaults followed by ###. 'unified' mode prefixes the default setting with a minus sign, followed by the changed setting prefixed with a plus sign

subcommands.name: makemessages

name: --locale
-l
options.description: Creates or updates the message files for the given locale(s) (e.g. pt_BR). Can be used multiple times

name: --exclude
-x
options.description: Locales to exclude. Default is none. Can be used multiple times

name: --domain
-d
options.description: The domain of the message files (default: "django")

name: --all
-a
options.description: Updates the message files for all existing locales

name: --extension
-e
options.description: The file extension(s) to examine (default: "html,txt,py", or "js" if the domain is "djangojs"). Separate multiple extensions with commas, or use -e multiple times

name: --symlinks
-s
options.description: Follows symlinks to directories when examining source code and templates for translation strings

name: --ignore
-i
options.description: Ignore files or directories matching this glob-style pattern. Use multiple times to ignore more

options.name: --no-default-ignore
options.description: Don't ignore the common glob-style patterns 'CVS', '.*', '*~' and '*.pyc'

options.name: --no-wrap
options.description: Don't break long message lines into several lines

options.name: --no-location
options.description: Don't write '#: filename:line' lines

options.name: --add-location
options.description: [{full,file,never}] Controls '#: filename:line' lines. If the option is 'full' (the default if not given), the lines include both file name and line number. If it's 'file', the line number is omitted. If it's 'never', the lines are suppressed (same as --no-location). --add-location requires gettext 0.19 or newer

suggestions.name: full
suggestions.description: The lines include both file name and line number

suggestions.name: file
suggestions.description: The line number is omitted

suggestions.name: never
suggestions.description: The lines are suppressed (same as --no-location)

options.name: --no-obsolete
options.description: Remove obsolete message strings

options.name: --keep-pot
options.description: Keep .pot file after making messages. Useful when debugging

name: --noinput
--no-input

options.description: Nominates a database to synchronize. Defaults to the "default" database

options.name: --fake
options.description: Mark migrations as run without actually running them

options.name: --fake-initial
options.description: Detect if tables already exist and fake-apply initial migrations if so. Make sure that the current database schema matches your initial migration before using this flag. Django will only check for an existing table name

options.description: Shows a list of the migration actions that will be performed

options.name: --run-syncdb
options.description: Creates tables for apps without migrations

options.name: --check
options.description: Exits with a non-zero status if unapplied migrations exist

options.description: When using plain Python, ignore the PYTHONSTARTUP environment variable and ~/.pythonrc.py script

name: -i
--interface
options.description: Specify an interactive interpreter interface. Available options: "ipython", "bpython", and "python"

options.description: Instead of opening an interactive shell, run a command as Django and exit

subcommands.description: Runs a Python interactive interpreter. Tries to use IPython or bpython, if one of them is available. Any standard input is executed as code

subcommands.name: showmigrations

options.name: --database
options.description: Nominates a database to synchronize. Defaults to the "default" database

name: --list
-l
options.description: Shows a list of all migrations and which are applied. With a verbosity level of 2 or above, the applied datetimes will be included

name: --plan
-p
options.description: Shows all migrations in the order they will be applied. With a verbosity level of 2 or above all direct migration dependencies and reverse dependencies (run_before) will be included

subcommands.name: test

name: --noinput
--no-input
options.description: Tells Django to NOT prompt the user for input of any kind

options.name: --failfast
options.description: Tells Django to stop running the test suite after first failed test

options.name: --testrunner
options.description: Tells Django to use specified test runner class instead of the one specified by the TEST_RUNNER setting

name: -t
--top-level-directory
options.description: Top level of project for unittest discovery

name: -p
--pattern
options.description: The test matching pattern. Defaults to test*.py

options.name: --keepdb
options.description: Preserves the test DB between runs

name: -r
--reverse
options.description: Reverses test cases order

options.name: --debug-mode
options.description: Sets settings.DEBUG to True

name: -d
--debug-sql
options.description: Prints logged SQL queries on failure

options.name: --parallel
options.description: Run tests using up to N parallel processes

options.name: --tag
options.description: Run only tests with the specified tag. Can be used multiple times

options.name: --exclude-tag
options.description: Do not run tests with the specified tag. Can be used multiple times

options.name: --pdb
options.description: Runs a debugger (pdb, or ipdb if installed) on error or failure

name: -b
--buffer
options.description: Discard output from passing tests

options.name: -k
options.description: Only run test methods and classes that match the pattern or substring. Can be used multiple times. Same as unittest -k option
